title: crambe hispanica subsp. glabrata
intro: Crambe hispanica subsp. glabrata, also known as sea kale, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crambe hispanica subsp. glabrata include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used as a food ingredient, particularly in traditional Mediterranean cuisine.
